_meta.definition: secured_gross_mw = energized + committed/under-construction gross facility power, MW. Speculative pipeline and pending interconnection EXCLUDED. Where the company discloses only critical IT load, converted x1.25 and flagged in basis.
_meta.refresh_rule: Refresh at quarterly rescore and on earnings (rule 9 pass). Used by batch_score.py to compute EV/MW for the Layer 9 capacity cohort (rule 13).
_meta.band_anchor: AI DC replacement cost ~$9-10M per gross MW (~$11-12M per IT MW), facility+power excl. IT gear

CRWV.basis: company 'contracted power', IT-vs-gross unspecified (treated gross, flagged)

NBIS.basis: company 'contracted power', IT-vs-gross unspecified (treated gross, flagged); >75% owned

APLD.basis: ~1,400 MW contracted critical IT across 5 AI Factory campuses (CoreWeave PF1 400 + IG-hyperscaler PF2 200 / Delta Forge 1 430 / Delta Forge 2 210) x1.25 = 1,750 gross-equiv + 286 MW legacy hosting gross (Jamestown 106 + legacy Ellendale 180) = 2,036; cross-check vs company-disclosed ~2.15 GW grid-connected utility power. All leases signed/financed (committed/under-construction). EXCLUDED as speculative: mgmt '5-6 GW view' + ~1 GW marketed development sites. BIG MOVE 1,036->2,036 (prior counted only 600 IT) -- flagged for Dom.
APLD.source: APLD Delta Forge 2 lease PR 2026-06-08 (~1.4 GW contracted IT, ~2.15 GW grid-connected, ~$36B backlog) + 8-K 2026-06-16 (ELN-04 150 MW notes); per-stock/APLD/context-2026-06-22.md

IREN.basis: Re-based off Q3 FY26 disclosure of 4,510 MW executed grid-connection across 7 sites. Included (energized + under-construction/committed): Childress 750 + Sweetwater 1 1,400 (energizing 2026) + Sweetwater 2 600 + Canal Flats 30 + Mackenzie 80 + Prince George 50 = 2,910. EXCLUDED: Oklahoma 1,600 (executed grid-connection but NOT yet under construction) per energized+committed standard; 800 MW Australia (2028) + broader 5.8 GW pipeline as speculative. Value coincidentally unchanged at 2,910. JUDGMENT CALL flagged for Dom: counting Oklahoma would lift to 4,510.
IREN.source: IREN Q3 FY26 Business Update & Results (8-K/10-Q period 2026-03-31), GlobeNewswire 2026-05-07; per-stock/IREN/context-2026-06-22.md

CORZ.basis: 1.9 GW gross utility power across 10 sites (1.3 GW leasable; CoreWeave 590 MW critical IT within it)

CIFR.basis: 207 MW Odessa mining (energized, gross) + 600 MW contracted HPC gross (Black Pearl ~300 AWS 15-yr + Barber Lake 300 Fluidstack/Google 10-yr) + Stingray 100 MW gross (Andrews TX, financed by $810M 6.000% notes, energize Q4 2026) = 907. RECONCILED double-count concern: prior basis text double-listed '3rd campus 100' AND 'Stingray 100', but the 907 sum counts Stingray ONCE and there is no separate 3rd campus -- no double-count. Business-update's 807 figure simply predates/excludes Stingray. 6 additional planned HPC sites EXCLUDED as speculative.
CIFR.source: CIFR Q1 2026 10-Q (period 2026-03-31) + business-update 8-K (807 MW op+contracted) + Stingray $810M notes 8-K 2026-06-15; per-stock/CIFR/context-2026-06-22.md

CLSK.basis: total contracted gross utility power, owned/leased sites; zero AI/HPC MW contracted to date

BTDR.basis: 1,744 MW online gross + ~735 committed (Clarington OH 570 utility-confirmed + Tydal ~165 in construction); company does not publish clean secured split
BTDR.source: Bitdeer April 2026 Production & Operations Update, May 12 2026

HUT.basis: 1,020 MW energized energy-under-management + River Bend 245 MW IT (~330 MW gross utility, under construction, financed by $3.25B notes) + Beacon Point 352 MW IT x1.25 = 440 gross-equiv (now SIGNED 15-yr lease, AA-+ tenant, financed by $4.25B 6.129% notes closed 2026-06-10) = 1,790. Beacon Point reclassified from prior 'development.' Cross-check: Q1-call '830 MW under construction' ~= River Bend + Beacon Point. EXCLUDED development pipeline: 550 dev + 1,680 exclusivity + 5,315 diligence. Move 1,350->1,790 flagged for Dom (IT->gross x1.25 assumption; '1,020 under management' includes managed-for-others).
HUT.source: Hut 8 Q1 2026 10-Q/8-K 2026-05-06 (597 MW IT signed take-or-pay) + Beacon Point $4.25B notes 8-K 2026-06-05/06-10; per-stock/HUT/context-2026-06-22.md

KEEL.basis: 341 MW energized + 430 MW secured (executed utility agreements), gross grid power; 1.5 GW expansion pipeline excluded

RIOT.basis: 1.7 GW fully ERCOT-approved gross across Rockdale ~700 + Corsicana ~1,000; AMD lease 50 MW critical IT within it (expanded 25->50, first 5 MW online Q1 2026). Kentucky 162 MW developed capacity (non-ERCOT) EXCLUDED per prior ERCOT-only scope -- including it gives ~1,862. Headline confirmed consistent with Q1 2026 10-Q; only AMD contracted, no hyperscale tenant signed (conversion = AI-Thesis's job, not Value, per rule 13).
RIOT.source: Riot 10-Q period 2026-03-31 (filed 2026-04-30) + Q1 2026 earnings deck; AMD lease 8-K 2026-01-16; per-stock/RIOT/context-2026-06-22.md

WULF.basis: 522 critical IT MW executed long-term leases (company FY2025 total: Core42/G42 60 IT energized Q1 2026 + Fluidstack Lake Mariner CB-3/4/5 + Abernathy TX 168, all under construction delivering 2026) x1.25 = 653 gross-equiv. Using the company's 522 total nets out the prior Fluidstack-360-vs-CB-buildings overlap (prior basis used 438 -> 550). Muskie KY (up to 1 GW, 2028/2030 ramp) EXCLUDED as development; Lake Mariner mining shares site power, not added.
WULF.source: TeraWulf Q4/FY2025 results PR (522 critical IT MW) + Q1 2026 10-Q (60 MW energized, period 2026-03-31); per-stock/WULF/context-2026-06-22.md

WYFI.basis: NC-1 54 MW Duke-energized gross + Enovum Montreal 16 MW (MTL-1/2/3); NC-1 +45 MW 2027 phase EXCLUDED (no binding construction contract — counting it gives 115 MW / $9.8M/MW / band 45 vs base 70 MW / $16.1M/MW / band 30 — STRADDLES BAND, flagged for Dom)
WYFI.source: WYFI Q1 2026 results PR + transcript; Nscale 40MW/$865M 8-K

SHAZ.basis: 102 MW contracted + ~30 other secured; ONLY ~2 MW ENERGIZED (~98% un-energized — unlike all cohort peers; EV/MW is a backlog bet, not an asset read). ESDS anchor counterparty solvency contested (Bleecker St short report)

HIVE.basis: 440 MW operating gross (Canada+Sweden+Paraguay, predominantly BTC mining; AI-grade critical IT energized <10 MW) + 100 MW Paraguay Yguazu Phase-3 under construction (commissioning Q3 2026). EXCLUDED as development: GTA Gigafactory 320 MW (announced 2026-05-18, H2 2027, $3.5B campus financing incomplete), Grand Falls 70 + Toronto Airport 7 (construction status unconfirmed) — same standard that excluded HUT's development tranche. Aggressive alternative incl. Canadian HPC pipeline = 840 MW (subagent's proposal, flagged).
HIVE.source: HIVE FY2026 results PR + DCD Yguazu 400MW expansion + GTA Gigafactory announcement (asof basis: FY2026 results FYE Mar 31; GTA Gigafactory announced 2026-05-18, excluded as development)
